Daily review of security events—for example, notifications or alerts that identify suspicious or anomalous activities—as well as logs from critical system components, and logs from systems that perform security functions, such as firewalls, IDS/IPS, file integrity monitoring (FIM) systems, etc., is necessary to identify potential issues. The determination of "security event" will vary for each organization and may include consideration for the type of technology, location, and function of the device. Organizations may also wish to maintain a baseline of "normal" traffic to help identify anomalous behavior. An entity that uses third-party service providers to perform log review services should ensure that a context is provided to the service provider, so it understands the entity's environment, policies, and procedures and can establish a baseline of "normal" traffic to help identify anomalous behavior, and can alert about anomalous events and provide information and context about the entity's environment.

Control Description: Automated mechanisms are used to perform audit log reviews.
Domain: Requirement 10: Log and Monitor All Access to System Components and Cardholder Data
Implementation Guidance: Manual log reviews are difficult to perform, even for one or two systems, due to the amount of log data that is generated. However, using log harvesting, parsing, and alerting tools, centralized log management systems, event log analyzers, and security information and event management (SIEM) solutions can help facilitate the process by identifying log events that need to be reviewed. Establishing a baseline of normal audit activity patterns is critical to the effectiveness of an automated review. Each entity should keep logging tools aligned with any changes in the established baselines, and should update any changes in its environment to reflect any change occurring in the established baselines. The entity should also keep logging tools updated to reflect any changes and updates. Effective log management solutions collect and format log data from disparate sources to facilitate analysis.

Control Description: Logs of all other system components (those not specified in Requirement 10.4.1) are reviewed periodically.
Domain: Requirement 10: Log and Monitor All Access to System Components and Cardholder Data
Implementation Guidance: Periodic review of logs for all other system components (not specified in Requirement 10.4.1) helps to identify indications of potential issues or attempts to access critical systems via less-critical systems.

Control Description: The frequency of periodic log reviews for all other system components (not defined in Requirement 10.4.1) is defined in the entity's targeted risk analysis, which is performed according to all elements specified in Requirement 12.3.1.
Domain: Requirement 10: Log and Monitor All Access to System Components and Cardholder Data
Implementation Guidance: Entities can determine the optimum period to review these logs based on criteria such as the complexity of each entity's environment, the number of types of systems that are required to be evaluated, and the functions of such systems.

Control Description: Exceptions and anomalies identified during the review process are addressed.
Domain: Requirement 10: Log and Monitor All Access to System Components and Cardholder Data
Implementation Guidance: If exceptions and anomalies identified during the log-review process are not investigated, the entity may be unaware of unauthorized and potentially malicious activities occurring within their network. Entities should consider how to address the following when defining and managing exceptions and anomalies: How to mark and prioritize exceptions and anomalies; How to escalate and report exceptions and anomalies, and escalable exceptions and anomalies, and procedures for escalation; Who is responsible for investigating and for any remediation tasks.

Control Description: Vulnerabilities are discovered continually by malicious individuals and researchers as well as being introduced by new software system components, processes and custom software change environment.
Domain: Requirement 11: Test Security Systems and Networks Regularly
Implementation Guidance: Vulnerabilities are discovered continually by malicious individuals and researchers as well as being introduced by new software system change environment. Refer to Appendix G for definitions of PCI DSS terms.

Control Description: All security policies and operational procedures are identified in Requirement 1.1. Documented. Kept up to date. In use. Known to all affected parties.
Domain: Requirements and Testing Procedures
Implementation Guidance: Requirement 1.1.1 is about effectively managing various policies and procedures specified throughout Requirement 1.1. It is important to ensure they are properly documented, maintained, and disseminated. It is important to ensure they are equally important to ensure they are properly documented, maintained, and disseminated.|Security policies define the security objectives and control objectives that drive the controls that follow and therefore controls have important to ensure they are disseminated and defined, and describe how to perform activities and describe how to perform activities and describe how to perform activities and describe how to perform activities and consistent manner and in accordance with policy.|It is important to update policies and procedures as needed and change occurs, and any and change occurs, and any change occurs and possible after a change occurs and possible after a change occurs and provide a control cycle.|Security policies define the security objectives and control objectives that drive the controls that follow and describe how to perform activities and define the controls that define the security objectives and describe how to perform activities and describe how to perform activities and consistent manner and in accordance with policy.

Control Description: Authorized and unauthorized wireless access points are managed as follows: • The presence of wireless access points is tested for wireless access points requirement • All authorized and non-identified wireless access points at testing location occurs at least one every three months • All authorized and non-identified wireless access points are tested for identity both authorized and unauthorized elements specified in the requirement • A unarmed monitoring is used to generated persons are
Domain: Requirement 1: Establish and Implement Network Segmentation
Implementation Guidance: Implementation and/or exploitation of wireless can prevent care common paths to the network. Unauthorized wireless access points are common paths for attackers to network to a network port to a network port to a network port to a network port to a network port to a network port to a network Control Description: Retain audit log history for at least 12 months, with at least the most recent three months immediately available for analysis.

Hardening controls to better defend the systems themselves becomes ineffective if logs are not maintained historically to allow timely identification and minimization impact of a data compromise.

Control Description: Processes and mechanisms for regularly testing security systems and networks are defined and understood. Wireless access points are identified and unauthorized wireless access points are addressed. External and internal vulnerabilities are regularly identified, prioritized and addressed. External and internal penetration testing is regularly performed and exploitable vulnerabilities are corrected. Network intrusions and unexpected file changes are detected and responded to. Unauthorized changes on payment pages are detected and responded to.
Domain: Requirement 11: Test Security Systems and Networks Regularly
Implementation Guidance: Processes and mechanisms for regularly testing security systems and networks are defined and understood. This requirement ensures that organizations proactively test and validate the security posture of their systems and networks to identify and remediate vulnerabilities before they can be exploited by malicious actors.

Control Description: Roles and responsibilities for performing activities are documented and assigned, and personnel may not be formally assigned, personnel may not be formally assigned undertake responsibilities
Domain: Requirement 11: Test Security of Systems and Networks
Implementation Guidance: If roles are not formally assigned, personnel may not be aware of their day-to-day responsibilities and critical activities may not occur. Good Practices: Roles and responsibilities may be documented within processes or maintained within separate documents. As part of commencing roles and responsibilities, entities can consider having personnel acknowledge their assigned roles and responsibilities. Examples: A method to document roles and responsibilities is to assign them to responsibility assignment matrix that includes responsible, accountable, consulted and informed (also called a RACI matrix).

Control Description: Wireless access points are identified and unauthorized wireless access points are addressed.
Domain: Requirement 11: Test Security Systems and Networks Regularly
Implementation Guidance: Wireless access points are identified and unauthorized wireless access points are addressed. This control ensures that organizations maintain visibility of wireless connectivity and prevent unauthorized access to the network through rogue or misconfigured wireless access points.

Control Description: An inventory of authorized wireless access points is maintained, including a documented business justification for each.
Domain: Requirement 11: Test Security of Systems and Cardholder Data Regularly
Implementation Guidance: An inventory of authorized wireless access points is maintained, including a documented business justification for each. These non-company devices are often treated as an untrusted network and may present a considerable threat to the cardholder data environment if not configured appropriately. A clean inventory ensures that only authorized wireless devices are allowed to connect to the network and helps prevent the connection of unauthorized devices that could be used to gain unauthorized access to CDE and cardholder data.

Control Description: External and internal penetration testing is regularly performed and exploitable vulnerabilities are corrected.
Domain: Requirement 11: Test Security Systems and Networks Regularly
Implementation Guidance: External and internal penetration testing is regularly performed and exploitable vulnerabilities are corrected. This control validates the effectiveness of security controls through simulated attacks and ensures that identified vulnerabilities are remediated.

Control Description: Examine penetration testing results to verify that noted vulnerabilities and security weaknesses are corrected in accordance with the requirement. • Accordance with the entity's assessment of the security issue as defined in Requirement 6.3.1. • Penetration testing is repeated to verify the corrections.
Domain: Requirement 11: Test Security of Systems and Networks
Implementation Guidance: The results of a penetration test are usually a prioritised list of vulnerabilities discovered by a penetration tester. Often a tester will have maintained a system component. For remediation the entity should consider how likely the vulnerability is to be exploited. Listing the entity's own vulnerability risk assessment process (see requirement 6.3.1) ensures the vulnerabilities that pose the highest risk to the entity will be remediated most quickly. As part of the entity assessment of risk entities should consider how likely the vulnerability is to be exploited and whether there are other controls in place to mitigate the risk. Any weaknesses assessment shall point to PCI DSS assessment requirements not being met should be addressed.

Control Description: When an entity uses segmentation controls to isolate the CDE from other networks, perform on-demand testing procedures are defined to test all segmentation controls/methods in accordance with PCI DSS requirements in this requirement.
Domain: Requirement 11: Test Security of Systems and Networks
Implementation Guidance: When an entity uses segmentation controls to isolate the CDE from internal unresolved networks, the security of the CDE is dependent on that isolation. Malfunctioning, missing tools and techniques available from the CDE part of the network isolation testing methodology controls involve the entity validating all segmentation controls/methods in accordance with PCI DSS requirement; testing methodologies validate that all elements specified in this requirement can then be applied. Techniques such as lost discovery and port scanning can be used to verify quote-scope assignments are in scope to the CDE.

Control Description: Additional requirement for multitenant service providers only: Examine evidence that multitenant service providers support their customers for external penetration testing per Requirement 11.4.3 and 11.4.4.
Domain: Requirement 11: Test Security of Systems and Networks
Implementation Guidance: Entities need to conduct penetration tests in accordance with PCI DSS to simulate attacker behavior and discover vulnerabilities in their networks and systems. Multitenant service providers must ensure their customers have the ability to conduct penetration testing because it is critical for customers to validate their own security controls. There are multitenant service providers that may not allow penetration testing on their part of the network or systems, which can put their customer's cardholder data at risk. This requirement applies to the multitenant service provider, not the customer, because the testing of the multitenant service provider is often necessary to validate the security of the customer's environment. Control Description: POI terminals using SSL and/or early TLS are confirmed as not susceptible to known SSL/early TLS exploits.
Domain: Appendix A2: Additional PCI DSS Requirements for Entities Using SSL/Early TLS for Card-Present POS POI
Implementation Guidance: This Appendix applies only to entities using SSL and/or early TLS for POS POI terminals. Entities using SSL and early TLS for POS POI terminals, including service providers that support entities, must work toward upgrading to a strong cryptography protocol as soon as possible. Additonally, SSL and/or early TLS must be included into environments where those protocols don't already exist. At the line of publication, the known exploits to SSL/early TLS are well documented. However, new vulnerabilities could emerge at any time. The PCI DSS requirements directly affected are as follows: • Requirement 2.2.5 - Where any resource services, protocols or ciphers are present, business justification is documented and the risk of using resource services, protocols, or ciphers is assessed, documented and implemented as a strong cryptography • Requirement 2.2.7 - All non-console administrative access is encrypted using strong cryptography • Requirement 4.2.1 - Striving cryptography and see protocols are implemented to safeguard PANs during transmission over public networks. SL and early TLS must be used as a security control to meet these requirements, except in the case of POS POI terminals. The following provisions are detailed in this Appendix: • New POS POI implementations must not use SSL or early TLS as a security control • POI terminal service providers must provide a secure service offering • Service providers supporting existing POS POI implementations that use SSL and/or early TLS must have a formal Risk Mitigation plan and the SSL termination points to which they connect may continue using SSL early TLS as a security control. Requirements in this Appendix are eligible for the Customized Approach.

Control Description: This Appendix applies only to entities designated by a payment brand and/or acquirer as requiring additional validation of existing PCI DSS requirements. A entity is required to undergo an assessment according to the Appendix ONLY if instructed to do so by an acquirer or a payment brand. Examples of entities the Appendix could apply include: These provision process and/or transmission large volumes of account data. These have suffered significant or repeat breaches of account data. Additionally other PCI standards may reference completion of this Appendix. These supplemental validation steps are intended to provide greater assurance that PCI DSS controls are maintained effectively and on a continuous basis through validation of business-as-usual (BAU) processes and scoring consideration.
Domain: Appendix A3: Designated Entities Supplemental Validation (DESV)

Control Description: Multi-tenant service providers protect and separate all customers' environments and data. The provider cannot access the provider's environment and the provider's customers' environments without authorization. Defined Approach Requirements: A4.1.1 Logical separation is implemented as follows: • The provider cannot access its customers' environment and data without authorization. • Customers cannot access the provider's environment and data without authorization.
Domain: Appendix A4: Additional PCI DSS Requirements for Multi-Tenant Service Providers
Implementation Guidance: All service providers are responsible for meeting PCI DSS requirements for their own environments as applicable to the services offered to their customers in their own right. Multi-tenant service providers that offer various shared data center services (such as physical or virtual servers) to customers may include but are not limited to hosting multiple entities on single server provisioning e-commerce services and processor services in a shared environment. Service providers that provide data centers (other called co-location or co-hosting providers) where equipment space and connectivity are provided to meet tenant service requirements are still responsible to comply with PCI DSS requirements for which responsibility is still responsible for which responsibility is still responsible for which responsibility is still responsible for which responsibility is still responsible for which responsibility is still responsible for which responsibility is still responsible for which responsibility is still responsible. Note: Even though a multi-tenant services provider may meet these requirements and customer is still responsible to comply with PCI DSS requirements for which responsibility is still responsible. Others there are applicable. Offers specific services to the environment and responsibility is shared between both customer and the customer and TPSP requirements are shared between both customer and TPSP. Purpose: Multi-tenant controls ensure that the provider's environment and the customer's environment are logically separated in such a way that customers cannot access the provider's environment without authorization, nor can the provider access its customers' environment without authorization. Good Practice: Providers should ensure strong separation between the environments and that shared infrastructure is not accessible to customers without authorization.
